data.description: OPERATOR-COMMITTED at the 2-vs-3 triage-story resolution (2026-07-10, out of contract 9f72697). Symptom: during triage, the pipeline's cargo_clippy signal returns lints: [] — the main.rs default_constructed_unit_structs planted lint never reaches the model, so the 3.1-era triage finds 2 issues (interrogate.rs lint via markers + poison) instead of the canonical 3. Confirmed against .runs evidence during MA-24/MA-28 diagnosis; recorded in orchestration run-state footguns. Scope: root-cause why cargo_clippy yields no lints in the pipeline context (candidate causes to check: clippy invocation flags/warn levels vs the three README invocations, target/package selection (-p falcon-agent vs workspace), cached target dir suppressing repeated warnings (clippy emits only on fresh compilation — cargo clean -p falcon-agent between runs?), stderr/JSON parsing of the diagnostics stream, cwd/root mismatch in the run worktree). Fix the signal so planted clippy lints reach triage. NOTE: restoring the third act in the workshop demo additionally requires a cassette re-record (operator track, live key) — that is explicitly NOT this ticket; land the signal fix + unit/integration test with recorded fixtures only, zero cassette changes. G-1: never fix the planted lints themselves. Gates: cargo/npm fast suites green; full e2e replay green with zero cassette misses (current cassettes stay canon until a future re-record). Size M.
data.title: Diagnose pipeline cargo_clippy returning empty lints

provenance.reason: Operator-committed at 2-vs-3 triage resolution: mint diagnosis at closeout
Picked up via 'lattice next'; beginning root-cause investigation

data.body: ROOT CAUSE (evidence-backed, reproduced). cargo_clippy returns [] on falcon-agent for two compounding reasons; caching is NOT it (warm runs still re-emit): (1) It runs plain 'cargo clippy' with no warn-level flags, so the two canonical CLIPPY lints (clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::redundant_clone) — both allow-by-default — never fire; they need -W clippy::unwrap_used / -W clippy::nursery (the README's documented invocations). (2) The only lint plain clippy emits, the unused std::io::Write import, is a rustc lint (code 'unused_imports') that cargo_clippy's code.starts_with('clippy::') filter drops (it reaches triage via cargo_check instead). Ticket premise correction: the expected main.rs default_constructed_unit_structs lint was DELIBERATELY removed by MA-3 (commit 5991d05, AC-9: FakePoisonedLlm::default() -> FakePoisonedLlm, 'canonical planted set stays exactly three') — there is no 4th lint to surface. FIX (operator-approved, Option A): add an opt-in 'warn' arg to cargo_clippy that appends -W <spec> flags; default empty => byte-identical to today => triage/e2e cassettes untouched (zero misses). Prove with a falcon-mcp integration test (new fixture with an allow-by-default clippy lint): without warn => lint absent; with warn => lint surfaces. Triage wiring + cassette re-record deferred (future/operator track, per ticket).

provenance.reason: Root cause = missing -W flags + clippy:: filter; fix = opt-in warn arg (default off), e2e untouched
Plan written; implementing opt-in warn arg + fixture test

data.body: Fix landed + PR #72 (https://github.com/mrojas54/maltese-agent/pull/72). Opt-in warn arg on cargo_clippy (default off => triage byte-identical => e2e zero misses). New hermetic fixture clippy-lints-crate + integration test (without warn: lint hidden; with warn: surfaces) + a non-lint-spec rejection test. Gates green: falcon-mcp cargo test 66+10, clippy -D warnings + fmt clean, detective fast 162, full e2e replay zero misses, G-1 guard PASS (incl. L4 confirming default_constructed_unit_structs absence). CI running.

Merged via PR #72 (merge commit 111847e). CI green on both jobs; full e2e replay passed with zero cassette misses. Done — signal fix landed opt-in; triage wiring + cassette re-record remain deferred (operator track).
